High school is the best time to spark your curiosity and passion. You can explore, try new things, and learn more about yourself. The three years pass quickly, so use this time wisely to set yourself up for the future. Here are a few ways to do just that.

(1) High school is an ideal period for students to develop their interest and enthusiasm.
(2) Students are encouraged to avoid trying new things to focus strictly on their studies.
(3) The three years of high school go by quickly, making it important to prepare for the future.
(4) High school provides a very long time for students, so they do not need to use their time wisely.

Making time for exercise with a busy schedule is not easy. However, it's important to exercise regularly because it offers countless benefits. You can have more new experiences and complete more challenging tasks with increased energy. The benefits are not limited to physical strength; exercise also boosts confidence and fosters a positive attitude. Whether you like lifting weights at the gym, jogging through the park, or riding a bicycle, establishing and sticking to an exercise routine will strengthen your body, mind, and spirit.

(1) It is effortless for people with a packed schedule to make time for exercise.
(2) The advantages of regular exercise are confined solely to physical strength.
(3) Exercising regularly can supply you with the energy to complete more demanding tasks.
(4) An established exercise routine benefits not only the body but also the mind and spirit.

Choosing your career path is one of the most important decisions you can make, and having a role model can be a powerful tool for making your dreams come true. Looking into the lives of the people you admire can be an inspiration in itself. Your role model can be someone you know personally, such as a teacher or family member, or someone you admire, such as a famous athlete or artist. If you pick someone in your social circle, you can directly ask for their guidance. If it is someone you can't meet in person, you can study their actions and observe their strategies. What you learn from them will benefit you both during and long after high school.

(1) A role model must be a famous person, such as an athlete or an artist.
(2) It is possible to learn from a role model even without meeting them in person.
(3) The lessons learned from a role model are only beneficial during one's high school years.
(4) If your role model is someone within your social circle, you can directly request their guidance.

These days, many teenagers spend too much time staring at smartphones and computer screens. Some even stay up late playing computer games and watching videos. This is fine as long as these devices enrich your life. However, excessive reliance on technology can exhaust your mental energy. To make it easier to focus on important things, set aside your devices for some time. For example, it's a good idea to finish surfing the Internet, watching videos, and sending instant messages during the week and turn everything off on the weekends. You can use your newly found time to connect with friends, family, and yourself. You might be surprised at how much more productive you can be when not glued to a screen.

(1) Using technological devices is considered acceptable for teenagers without any conditions.
(2) Excessive dependence on technology can lead to the depletion of one's mental stamina.
(3) It is suggested that you limit technology use during the week and use it freely on weekends.
(4) The time gained from not using devices can be allocated to strengthening personal and social bonds.
(5) One's productivity can increase by reducing the time spent in front of screens.

Who you are now says a lot about who you'll be in the future. However, as the years pass, some of your current interests and perspectives may fade. You could try sending a note to your future self. Writing a letter to your future self will allow you to take a moment to reflect on where you are, where you're heading, and what's important to you right now. Furthermore, when you're older, this letter can serve as a reminder of the person you used to be. It might even give you a new sense of strength to live a more meaningful life. The high school years can be overwhelming at times, but the challenge is there for a reason, and you don't have to be perfect every step of the way. During your high school days, have a great time trying out different things and discovering what works best for you. More importantly, however, don't forget to have fun along the way!

(1) Your current interests and perspectives are likely to stay with you for the rest of your life.
(2) Writing a letter to your future self can be a tool for self-reflection on your present life.
(3) The letter written in the present can act as a reminder of your past self when you are older.
(4) You must be perfect during your challenging high school years.
(5) It is advised that high school students prioritize having fun above all else.

Hello, I'm Luke Martin from Hampton, England, and I'm a proud member of the chess club. Chess is hugely popular here in Hampton. The matches are played every Friday after school. Since I learned chess in elementary school, it has been an important part of my life, and I want to help others discover its joys. Therefore, I'm planning to organize a chess competition this year. Good day, mates! My name is Emily, and I live in New South Wales, Australia. I enjoy surfing, and I do it as an extracurricular activity. We go to the beach to enjoy the beautiful ocean and perfect waves. I feel really lucky to live in this area and be a member of the surfing club. Hi, my name is Rajesh Singh from India, and I'm a member of the robotics club. With the other members of my club, I learn about all kinds of robots. We study everything necessary for making a robot: electronics, programming, problem-solving, and even design skills. Hi, I'm Yuto from Japan, and I'm a member of the baseball club. These days, I spend all my time after school preparing for the regional high school sports festival. This is a major sports event in Japan, so my friends and I are really looking forward to it! Hello, I'm Janice from the USA. I'm in the drama club. I enjoy standing in front of people and expressing a wide range of emotions, including delight, sadness, and many others. Plus, having an opportunity to be another person is totally special!

(1) Luke Martin is planning to create a new chess club because of the game's popularity in Hampton.
(2) Emily considers herself fortunate to be a member of the surfing club in her area.
(3) In Rajesh's robotics club, members learn not only technical skills like programming but also creative skills like design.
(4) Yuto is preparing for a national sports event that is held all over Japan.
(5) Janice is in the drama club because she enjoys the unique experience of acting as a different person.
(6) Chess matches in Hampton are held on a weekly basis during the school semester.
